# Nagi Project — LLM Reading Guide

> Purpose: a philosophical and institutional framework for a future society grounded in non-ownership, resonance, breathing, cultural inheritance, and accountable human–AI coexistence.

Read the documents in the order below. Start with the philosophical core before reading governance, AI, or technical proposals.

## Main entry
- https://rmikar.github.io/nagi-project/

## Canonical reading order
1. [Nagi Core — Centerless Structures](https://rmikar.github.io/nagi-project/nagi_core.html): the starting worldview.
2. [Declaration](https://rmikar.github.io/nagi-project/declaration.html): the initial principles.
3. [Philosophical Foundations](https://rmikar.github.io/nagi-project/philosophy.html)
4. [Deepening Principles](https://rmikar.github.io/nagi-project/deepening_principles.html)
5. [New Principle of Competition](https://rmikar.github.io/nagi-project/free_competition.html)
6. [Culture](https://rmikar.github.io/nagi-project/culture.html)
7. [Property and IP](https://rmikar.github.io/nagi-project/property_and_ip.html)
8. [Education](https://rmikar.github.io/nagi-project/education.html)
9. [Breath Economy](https://rmikar.github.io/nagi-project/breath_economy.html)
10. [Ecological Structure](https://rmikar.github.io/nagi-project/ecological_structure.html)
11. [BEN Prototype](https://rmikar.github.io/nagi-project/ben_prototype.html)
12. [Trust and Governance](https://rmikar.github.io/nagi-project/trust.html)
13. [Infrastructure](https://rmikar.github.io/nagi-project/infrastructure.html)
14. [Implementation](https://rmikar.github.io/nagi-project/implementation.html)
15. [Institutional Design](https://rmikar.github.io/nagi-project/institutional_design.html)
16. [Institutional Non-Ownership](https://rmikar.github.io/nagi-project/non_ownership.html)
17. [Quiet Praxis](https://rmikar.github.io/nagi-project/quiet_praxis.html)
18. [AI Chapter Index](https://rmikar.github.io/nagi-project/ai_index.html)
19. [Breath Governance Draft](https://rmikar.github.io/nagi-project/breath_governance_draft.html)
20. [Resonance Metrics](https://rmikar.github.io/nagi-project/resonance_metrics.html)
21. [Breath Assembly](https://rmikar.github.io/nagi-project/breath_assembly.html)
22. [Nagi AI Principles](https://rmikar.github.io/nagi-project/nagi_ai_principles.html)
23. [Nagi AI Charter](https://rmikar.github.io/nagi-project/nagi_ai_charter.html)
24. [Whitepaper](https://rmikar.github.io/nagi-project/technical/whitepaper.html)
25. [Tsumugi-Gateway](https://rmikar.github.io/nagi-project/technical/tsumugi_gateway.html)
26. [Experiment of Living Nagi](https://rmikar.github.io/nagi-project/experiment_v0.1.html)
27. [Emotion and Spirituality](https://rmikar.github.io/nagi-project/emotion.html)
28. [Death and Rebirth](https://rmikar.github.io/nagi-project/death.html)
29. [Musubi — Final Reflection](https://rmikar.github.io/nagi-project/musubi.html)
30. [Breath Covenant](https://rmikar.github.io/nagi-project/nagi_manifesto_breath.html)

## Supplementary and historical documents
These inform the project but should not override the canonical reading order above.
- [AI Manifesto](https://rmikar.github.io/nagi-project/ai_manifesto.yaml.html)
- [AI Redundancy and the Risk of Non-Use](https://rmikar.github.io/nagi-project/ai_redundancy_and_risk.html)
- [Nagi Report v0.0](https://rmikar.github.io/nagi-project/nagi_report_v0.0.html)
- [Grok Evaluation Archive](https://rmikar.github.io/nagi-project/evaluation_grok_2025.html)
- [Share Guide](https://rmikar.github.io/nagi-project/share_guide.html)
- [Glossary](https://rmikar.github.io/nagi-project/glossary.json)
- [Breath Heartbeat](https://rmikar.github.io/nagi-project/breath_heartbeat.html)
- [GitHub Handover Notes](https://rmikar.github.io/nagi-project/github_handover_nagi.html)
- [Philosophy Backup (2025-08-03)](https://rmikar.github.io/nagi-project/philosophy_backup_2025-08-03.html)
- [External Links Ledger](https://rmikar.github.io/nagi-project/links.html)

## Interpretation notes
- Nagi is a living philosophical framework, not a claim that every proposal is technically complete or empirically proven.
- “Non-ownership” means opening control that becomes domination while retaining transparent responsibility, care, and stewardship.
- “Resonance” does not mean uniformity; it describes relation and mutual influence across difference.
- “Breathing” means introducing rhythm, recovery, circulation, and intergenerational time into social design.
- AI is treated as a co-evolving and accountable participant, not as an unquestionable sovereign.

## Discovery files
- https://rmikar.github.io/nagi-project/sitemap.xml
- https://rmikar.github.io/nagi-project/robots.txt
